page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-24xCPU-256GB is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a KVM hypervisor. It features 24 shared vCPUs utilizing AMD EPYC 7542 processors clocked at 2.0 GHz, with 24 physical cores and a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 10.67 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results show a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is in the bottom 10%, and Passmark memory scores are in the bottom 25%. However, multi-core performance is more robust, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores falling into the middle 50% average. Notably, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performs strongly, placing in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth for both read and write operations remains consistently in the average range.

The shared CPU allocation and lower single-core performance present a tradeoff for latency-sensitive single-threaded tasks, as evidenced by poor text generation speeds in LLM inference benchmarks. However, the generous 256.0 GB memory capacity offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated local storage or high-frequency single-core execution. This server is suited for multi-threaded data processing, memory-cached databases, static web serving, and general-purpose workloads requiring large memory footprints.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-24xCPU-256GB is an x86_64 server featuring 24 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM, operating on a KVM hypervisor. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate a significant tradeoff: single-core CPU performance and memory scores are relatively weak, placing in the bottom 10% and 25% respectively, while multi-core performance and memory bandwidth consistently reach average levels. Multi-threaded operations, such as bzip3 decompression, show strong performance in the top 25%. Given its high memory-to-core ratio of 10.67 GB per core, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy, multi-threaded workloads. It is best suited for memory-cached databases, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing tasks that do not depend on high single-core speeds or local storage.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-24xCPU-256GB is a KVM-based x86_64 server with 24 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 256 GB RAM. It offers strong multi-threaded performance but exhibits lower single-core speeds.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-24xCPU-256GB is a KVM server with 24 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 256 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive multi-threaded tasks.
tagline: A high-memory, multi-core virtual server designed for memory-intensive workloads and parallel data processing without local storage requirements.
bullet_points: 24 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs on x86_64 architecture
256.0 GB of RAM providing 10.67 GB per core
Strong multi-threaded performance, including top 25% bzip3 decompression
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for memory-cached databases and multi-threaded data processing
